display.purpose: Entry-detail titles only — the largest type in the system.

headline.purpose: Section headings; deliberately body-sized, weight and position do the work.

label.purpose: Type badges and sidebar keys — the only uppercase in the system.

mono.purpose: File paths, source ids, inline code, kbd hints. Accent voice only, never the layout voice.

motion.name: state-feedback
motion.value: color .15s ease, background-color .15s ease, border-color .15s ease
motion.purpose: Hover/focus feedback on interactive elements. The only transitions in the system.

motion.name: skel-pulse
motion.purpose: Skeleton rows while semantic search is in flight. Flattened under prefers-reduced-motion.

components.name: Ledger Row
components.description: The signature list item: a hairline ledger line — fixed date column, badge, title, score.
components.html: 2026-07-011on11:1 with Jane — growth plan0.843Agreed on the staff-eng track; revisit scope after Q3 planning.
components.css: .ds-lrow { display: grid; grid-template-columns: 92px minmax(0,1fr); gap: 14px; padding: 13px 4px; border-bottom: 1px solid var(--grid, #e1e0d9); text-decoration: none; color: var(--ink, #0b0b0b); font: 15px/1.55 system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-lrow:hover { background: var(--surface, #fcfcfb); } .ds-ldate { font-size: 12.5px; color: var(--muted, #6e6d66); font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; padding-top: 2px; } .ds-ltop { display: flex; align-items: baseline; gap: 10px; flex-wrap: wrap; } .ds-badge { font-size: 11px; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 0.04em; text-transform: uppercase; color: var(--ink-2, #52514e); border: 1px solid var(--grid, #e1e0d9); border-radius: 6px; padding: 1px 6px; } .ds-ltitle { font-weight: 600; font-size: 15px; } .ds-score { font-size: 12px; color: var(--accent-text, #1c60b4); font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; margin-left: auto; } .ds-lsnippet { display: block; font-size: 13px; color: var(--ink-2, #52514e); margin-top: 4px; }

components.name: Search Input
components.description: The primary control — recall starts here. Focus is a quiet border swap to Working Blue.
components.css: .ds-input-search { width: 100%; font: 15px/1.55 system-ui, sans-serif; color: var(--ink, #0b0b0b); background: var(--surface, #fcfcfb); border: 1px solid var(--border, rgba(11,11,11,0.1)); border-radius: 10px; padding: 10px 14px; outline: none; } .ds-input-search:focus, .ds-input-search:focus-visible { border-color: var(--accent, #2a78d6); }

components.name: Chip
components.description: Pill for people, teams, and tags; count in muted tabular numerals.
components.css: .ds-chip { font: 12.5px/1.55 system-ui, sans-serif; color: var(--ink-2, #52514e); background: var(--surface-2, #f0f0ed); border: 1px solid var(--border, rgba(11,11,11,0.12)); border-radius: 999px; padding: 3px 11px; cursor: pointer; } .ds-chip:hover, .ds-chip:focus-visible { color: var(--ink, #0b0b0b); border-color: var(--baseline, #c3c2b7); } .ds-chip .ds-n { color: var(--muted, #6e6d66); font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; margin-left: 5px; } .ds-chip[aria-pressed="true"] { color: var(--accent-text, #1c60b4); border-color: var(--accent, #2a78d6); }

components.name: Type Badge
components.description: Unboxed entry-type mark — letterspaced uppercase text, no border. The only uppercase in the system.
components.css: .ds-type-badge { font: 600 10.5px/1.55 system-ui, sans-serif; letter-spacing: 0.08em; text-transform: uppercase; color: var(--muted, #6e6d66); }

components.name: Nav Link (active)
components.description: Header navigation; active page gets a surface fill and inset hairline ring.
components.css: .ds-nav { display: flex; gap: 4px; font: 14px/1.55 system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-nav a { text-decoration: none; color: var(--ink-2, #52514e); padding: 4px 10px; border-radius: 6px; } .ds-nav a:hover, .ds-nav a:focus-visible { color: var(--ink, #0b0b0b); } .ds-nav a[aria-current="page"] { color: var(--ink, #0b0b0b); font-weight: 600; background: var(--surface, #fcfcfb); box-shadow: inset 0 0 0 1px var(--border, rgba(11,11,11,0.1)); }

components.name: Status Dot
components.description: Index status: colored dot + muted text — passive status never outshouts content.
components.html: indexed 26/26 2 stale — run memory index
components.css: .ds-status-ok, .ds-status-warn { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; font: 12.5px/1.55 system-ui, sans-serif; color: var(--muted, #6e6d66); font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; } .ds-status-ok::before, .ds-status-warn::before { content: ""; width: 7px; height: 7px; border-radius: 999px; } .ds-status-ok::before { background: var(--good, #0ca30c); } .ds-status-warn::before { background: var(--warn-text, #8a5a00); }

components.name: Colophon
components.description: Overview summary line — the ledger states its totals inline, it does not dashboard them.
components.html: 26 entries·45 people·81 tags·2026-06-28 → 2026-07-01
components.css: .ds-colophon { display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; align-items: baseline; gap: 6px 10px; font: 14px/1.55 system-ui, sans-serif; color: var(--ink-2, #52514e); font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; } .ds-colophon b { font-weight: 650; color: var(--ink, #0b0b0b); } .ds-colophon .ds-sep { color: var(--baseline, #c3c2b7); }

components.name: Tooltip
components.description: The one 'elevated' element — inverted ink instead of a shadow.
components.css: .ds-tooltip { display: inline-block; background: var(--ink, #0b0b0b); color: var(--page, #f9f9f7); border-radius: 8px; padding: 6px 10px; font: 12.5px/1.4 system-ui, sans-serif; max-width: 260px; } .ds-tooltip .ds-tval { font-weight: 650; } .ds-tooltip .ds-tlabel { opacity: 0.75; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; }

narrative.overview: This is a private book of record. One person opens it mid-workday to find what he knows about a colleague, a decision, a month — reads the entry, and closes it. The interface behaves like a well-kept ledger: dated rows in a single prose-width column, tabular numerals for every date and count, hairline rules instead of boxes, and one ink color that isn't black. Chrome recedes; the record carries the page. The system is deliberately unspectacular: typography does the hierarchy work — weight and size, never color blocks or decoration. Surfaces separate from the page by a single tone step and a 1px border; nothing floats, glows, or animates.
keyCharacteristics: Single centered column (max 880px), prose capped at 62ch
Warm-neutral paper with one accent: Working Blue, ≤10% of any screen
Flat construction: 1px borders and tone steps, zero shadows
Entries render as hairline ledger rows — fixed 92px date column, month-grouped; cards are reserved for charts
Tabular numerals on every date, count, and score
Dark-first: dark is the designed-primary theme (header toggle, localStorage); light inherits through the same tokens; dark ink is off-white, never pure #fff
Motion is feedback-only: 150ms color/border transitions and a quiet skeleton pulse; prefers-reduced-motion flattens all of it

rules.name: The One Ink Rule
rules.body: Working Blue is the only accent on any screen and covers ≤10% of it. Navigation state, links, scores, and data marks all share it. A second accent hue is prohibited.

rules.name: The Ink-on-Its-Own-Paper Rule
rules.body: Semantic states (good/warn) are dark text on a faint tint of the same hue — never white-on-saturated badges.

rules.name: The Tabular Numerals Rule
rules.body: Every date, count, and score renders with font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums. A ledger's numbers align.

rules.name: The Label Heading Rule
rules.body: Section headings are Label voice — 12px uppercase, +0.06em tracking, Muted — set apart by case, never by size. Only an entry title may exceed body size.

rules.name: The Flat Book Rule
rules.body: No box-shadows, no blurs, no glassmorphism. If an element must read as 'above', invert its colors (like the tooltip) — don't lift it.

rules.name: The Square Baseline Rule
rules.body: Chart bars are solid Working Blue with rounded caps at the data end only — square at the baseline.

dos: Do keep Working Blue at ≤10% of any screen; it must always mean something (link, focus, score, data).
Do use font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums on every date, count, and score.
Do separate surfaces with a 1px rgba(ink, 0.1) border and a single tone step — that is the entire elevation vocabulary.
Do show provenance plainly: mono file paths, source ids, and dates on every entry, in Muted or Ink Secondary.
Do define every color once per theme as a CSS custom property; dark mode remaps tokens, never restyles components.
Do hold body text at AA contrast (≥4.5:1) in both themes — long reading sessions are the norm.
Do give every interactive element a visible :focus-visible ring (2px Working Blue, 2px offset) — keyboard is a first-class input.
Do use Working Blue Text (never base Working Blue) whenever the accent is read as type.
donts: Don't ship SaaS dashboard clichés (PRODUCT.md, verbatim): no KPI hero cards, gradient accents, icon-tile grids, stat deltas, or 'analytics product' styling.
Don't ship dev-tool terminal cosplay (PRODUCT.md, verbatim): no scanlines, all-monospace layouts, or neon-on-black. Monospace is an accent voice only.
Don't add box-shadows, blurs, or glassmorphism anywhere. The Flat Book Rule has no exceptions.
Don't introduce a second accent hue, fill the type badges, or color entry titles.
Don't use border-left stripes as category markers — entry types are marked by the outline badge.
Don't add entrance animations, staggered reveals, or skeleton shimmer. If motion is ever added, it must explain a state change and respect prefers-reduced-motion.
